race.name: Tervahiihto
race.tagline: Seventy kilometers from Oulu since 1889 — the world's oldest continuously held cross-country ski race, born in the tar-trading capital of Finland's frozen north.

vitals.aid_stations: 5-6 stations with warm drinks, Finnish pastries, and energy food

climate.description: February in Oulu at 65°N latitude delivers deep, reliable winter. The Gulf of Bothnia coastline moderates temperatures slightly compared to inland Finland, but conditions remain firmly below freezing. Daylight is increasing from the polar winter minimum but still limited. Snow cover is consistent and deep.
challenges: Northern latitude means limited February daylight for back-of-pack skiers
Coastal winds from the Gulf of Bothnia can create wind chill
Flat terrain means sustained effort without recovery descents
70km distance at sub-minus-10 demands careful cold management

course.primary: Predominantly flat to gently rolling course through the forests and coastal lowlands around Oulu. The route traverses boreal pine forest and open marshland near the Gulf of Bothnia. The flat terrain rewards endurance and double-poling strength over technical ability.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails through boreal forest and coastal terrain
course.grooming: Good Finnish grooming standards with well-set classic and skate tracks
features: World's oldest continuously held cross-country ski race (since 1889)
Course through the boreal forests near the Gulf of Bothnia
'Terva' means tar — Oulu was historically Finland's tar-trading capital
Strong northern Finnish skiing culture and community
Flat terrain rewards aerobic fitness and double-poling power

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Maximum prestige as the world's oldest continuously held XC ski race — 130+ years of unbroken tradition since 1889. Perfect snow reliability and distance scores. The flat coastal terrain limits scenery and technicality scores. Strong Finnish national competitive depth.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Prestige 5 with score < 75 caps at TIER 2
